strong_descriptor: Independently designs services with clear API contracts, data models, and explicit consistency and failure-mode tradeoffs.
average_descriptor: Designs standard service architectures and is aware of scaling, but reasons about failure modes only at a surface level.
weak_descriptor: Implements features inside an existing design with little independent architecture ownership.
red_flags: no ownership of a system end to end

strong_descriptor: Has shipped systems handling real load; reasons concretely about partitioning, caching, queues, and back-pressure.
average_descriptor: Understands horizontal scaling and caching conceptually with some production exposure.
weak_descriptor: Limited exposure beyond a single-instance app; scaling described only in generic terms.

strong_descriptor: Writes well-factored, tested code; sets testing standards and reviews others' code rigorously.
average_descriptor: Writes reasonable code with unit tests for core paths; participates in reviews.
weak_descriptor: Tests are sparse or absent; relies on manual verification.

strong_descriptor: Owns services in production: on-call, incident response, observability, and measurable reliability improvements.
average_descriptor: Has supported production systems and handled some incidents under guidance.
weak_descriptor: Primarily worked in pre-production or hand-off model with no production ownership.
red_flags: never been on-call or owned uptime

strong_descriptor: Designs and ships well-structured REST or RPC services independently with clean interfaces.
average_descriptor: Builds endpoints to spec with occasional guidance on interface design.
weak_descriptor: Only modifies existing endpoints; has not designed a service interface.
red_flags: no service or API work shown

strong_descriptor: Consistently writes tested, readable code and improves the codebase opportunistically.
average_descriptor: Writes working code with tests on main paths.
weak_descriptor: Code works but is untested or hard to follow.

strong_descriptor: Systematically isolates root causes across the stack using logs, traces, and reproduction.
average_descriptor: Resolves most bugs with some trial and error.
weak_descriptor: Struggles to debug beyond the immediate symptom.
red_flags: no examples of debugging non-trivial issues

strong_descriptor: Communicates clearly, unblocks teammates, and gives constructive review feedback.
average_descriptor: Works well within a team and responds to feedback.
weak_descriptor: Works mostly in isolation with limited collaboration signals.
red_flags: no team or cross-functional work mentioned

strong_descriptor: Solid command of semantic HTML, modern CSS layout, and core JavaScript including async patterns.
average_descriptor: Comfortable with the basics and able to build simple pages independently.
weak_descriptor: Gaps in fundamentals; relies heavily on copied snippets.
red_flags: cannot explain basic layout or async behavior

strong_descriptor: Builds clean, reusable React components with correct state and props handling.
average_descriptor: Builds working React components with some structural rough edges.
weak_descriptor: Has only followed tutorials; limited independent React work.
red_flags: no hands-on React or component framework experience

strong_descriptor: Translates designs into pixel-accurate, responsive interfaces.
average_descriptor: Implements designs closely with minor inconsistencies.
weak_descriptor: Output diverges noticeably from the intended design.
red_flags: no evidence of building real user interfaces

strong_descriptor: Demonstrates fast learning, curiosity, and clear growth from feedback (projects, courses, internships).
average_descriptor: Shows reasonable progression and willingness to learn.
weak_descriptor: Little evidence of recent learning or growth.
red_flags: no projects, internships, or learning signals

strong_descriptor: Designs and owns robust CI/CD pipelines and automates release and rollback safely.
average_descriptor: Maintains existing pipelines and adds automation with guidance.
weak_descriptor: Limited pipeline experience; mostly manual deploys.

strong_descriptor: Manages infrastructure declaratively (Terraform, etc.) with reviewable, versioned modules.
average_descriptor: Uses IaC for common resources with some manual steps.
weak_descriptor: Provisions infrastructure mostly by hand through consoles.

strong_descriptor: Builds metrics, logging, and alerting; leads incident response and writes postmortems.
average_descriptor: Sets up basic monitoring and participates in incidents.
weak_descriptor: Minimal observability; reactive to outages.

strong_descriptor: Operates containerized workloads on Kubernetes or equivalent at production scale.
average_descriptor: Runs containers in cloud with working knowledge of orchestration.
weak_descriptor: Basic cloud usage; little container or orchestration depth.

strong_descriptor: Sets a clear, evidence-backed product strategy and connects it to business outcomes.
average_descriptor: Contributes to strategy and understands the broader roadmap.
weak_descriptor: Executes features without a clear strategic frame.

strong_descriptor: Aligns engineering, design, and leadership; navigates conflict and drives decisions.
average_descriptor: Works effectively with cross-functional partners on most issues.
weak_descriptor: Limited cross-functional influence; defers most decisions.

strong_descriptor: Defines metrics, runs experiments, and makes decisions grounded in data.
average_descriptor: Uses metrics to inform decisions with some rigor.
weak_descriptor: Decisions are mostly intuition with little measurement.

strong_descriptor: Consistently ships meaningful product outcomes on realistic timelines.
average_descriptor: Delivers committed work with occasional slippage.
weak_descriptor: Delivery is inconsistent or unclear.

strong_descriptor: Writes clear, well-scoped requirements and user stories that engineering can build from directly.
average_descriptor: Writes usable requirements that sometimes need clarification.
weak_descriptor: Requirements are vague or frequently reworked.

strong_descriptor: Communicates crisply with partners and keeps everyone aligned on status and tradeoffs.
average_descriptor: Communicates adequately with the immediate team.
weak_descriptor: Communication gaps cause avoidable confusion.

strong_descriptor: Prioritizes effectively against value and effort and defends the tradeoffs.
average_descriptor: Prioritizes reasonably with some guidance.
weak_descriptor: Struggles to prioritize; treats everything as urgent.

strong_descriptor: Defines and tracks the right metrics and turns them into product actions.
average_descriptor: Reads standard product metrics and draws basic conclusions.
weak_descriptor: Little use of metrics in the role.
